editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter of condolence to Nancy O'Rahilly (1875-1961). She was a member of the Provisional Council of Cumann na mBan at the founding in 1914 and the wife of Michael Joseph O'Rahilly, self-described as 'The' O'Rahilly (1875-1916). The O'Rahilly fought in the Easter Rising and was shot in Parnell/Moore Street on 28 April 1916 and died some time later. Nancy was pregnant at the time of her husband's death – the child was born three months later. The writer asks for Mrs. O'Rahilly's sons ans says that some day they will remember their father with pride and gratitude, and prays for the comfort and protection of the O'Rahilly family. The writer also asks after a friend, 'small Desmond', and his wife.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
8 Buchanan Avenue
Bournemouth
Hants. 18.V1916. Dear Mrs. O'Rahilly You have been
much in my thoughts lately, & I
wouldhave written to you except that
I had not your address. How are the dear boys? Some
day when the present sense of
loss is less acute they will
remember with pride and gratitude
their Father's Devotion & self-sacrifice. May God comfort & protect you
and them. I hope we shall meet I have
for you , but may
get back to Ireland this summer;
do your sons? see my friend small
Desmond & his wee sisters?
I have had no news of them or
their parents for a long time
If you see "Dens" kiss him from
me. Yrs. with Sympathy
